primary_sources.use: DSSE pre-authentication encoding and envelope verification boundary
identity-bound verification, payload-digest checks, and transparency-log verification

finding: The existing DSSE promotion classifier recorded transparency_log_verified but did not require it. A verifier result with a trusted identity, matching payload digest and type, and valid PAE could therefore be promoted while transparency evidence was false or absent.
design_implication: Cryptographic evidence promotion now requires transparency-log verification by default. A separate trust model may disable that gate only through an explicit boolean false; string or implicit coercions fail closed.

test_cases_added: explicit false permits a separately declared non-transparency trust model

claim_state.observed: The previous classifier did not gate trusted signer counting on transparency_log_verified.
claim_state.implemented: The default gate now requires transparency_log_verified === true before a signer contributes to threshold.
not_proven: The Node test file has executed in CI.
Any real signature, certificate chain, Rekor inclusion proof, or signed-entry timestamp has been cryptographically verified by this module.
The underlying evidence claim is true.

evidence_strength: source-level implementation with deterministic tests committed; runtime test execution unavailable
falsification_route: Run the committed test file and additionally provide verifier results with false, missing, or string-valued transparency policy data. Any default-policy acceptance of such a result falsifies the implementation claim.
privacy_boundary: Public specifications and public repository source only; no private chat, health material, credentials, or identifying personal data included.
